summary: Sub-agents lack SendMessage in toolkit (spawn-task-return-result, not continuous peers); ~60-70 tool-use ceiling per dispatch; bound dispatches to ~1 component / 30-50min; commit-between-deliverables discipline; chunked Write+Edit append pattern for >32K HTML deliverables; transient API errors recover via identical-prompt retry; 64K output-token cap requires JSON+HTML split; W3.5 chapter-gen via `run-with-pg.mjs refresh --fast --quiet` belongs in the dispatch deliverable list (v709 codification)

trigger: Planning a non-degree-advancing milestone; converting accumulated social-rule debt into deterministic gates; meta-testing newly-introduced gates at ship time
summary: Counter-cadence cleanup-mission pattern productive every ~30 forward milestones; gate-not-vigilance discipline (convert offending rule to a gate, not a re-emphasized prose rule); meta-test newly-installed gates against the milestone's own release-notes during T14; discipline-as-code in 3-cluster lifecycle; cluster-close forward-notes are load-bearing decisions; phantom-deps npm-audit fix + probe threshold gap; finer-grained ~5-1-1 alternation as the steady-state maintenance complement to 30-ship batched cleanup (#10430)
codified_at_milestone: v1.49.654 (new domain — codified from v1.49.585-651 lesson cluster); finer-cadence pattern appended v1.49.805 (S7 lever)

trigger: Before executing any REVIEW ledger entry or planning artifact written as a sweep summary; when authoring audit rules and audit verdicts
summary: Per-file recon (~15min) precedes per-file code (verify scope, framing, prior-art). Audit rules classify by BEHAVIOR (e.g. `node:fs` import presence), not by filename pattern. Interface-conformance audits report a 3-way verdict: ALREADY CONFORMS / SHOULD CONFORM / INTENTIONALLY DIFFERENT (third verdict is load-bearing — prevents future re-flagging). REVIEW ledger fields distinguish filename-collisions (rename-scale) from class-name-collisions (merge-scale).
codified_at_milestone: v1.49.784 (new domain — codified from v1.49.780-782 lesson cluster)

trigger: Introducing a chokepoint, interface, or generator-template to N existing modules with high call-site multiplicity; authoring a generator that may run on partial input; adding the SECOND instance of a class-typed family
summary: Optional `ctx?` parameter pattern for chokepoint retrofit — three states (undefined→legacy permissive; defaultLoaderContext()→audit-only; restricted→enforced); zero call-site churn. Tolerant generators emit skip-the-line over `UNKNOWN`/`TODO`/placeholder sentinels — placeholders pollute output, break round-trip, and mask the schema gap. Exception: emit the default when the field has a load-bearing default value. Cross-class registries extract at the SECOND class instance (not first, not third) — the contrast between two real instances determines the registry shape; deferring to the third class ships a temporary wrong-source measure that has to be unwound. Production-caller scope-reduction via path-narrowing (#10440): when a forward-flag names a wrapper class but the underlying path is directly callable, call the path directly — wrapper-class instantiation accretes peer-dependency surface that is not load-bearing for the path under test. Size-ascending chip-pick reveals wire-shape diversity (#10444): when chipping down a KNOWN_UNWIRED allowlist, pick entries smallest-LOC first — file size correlates with structural complexity, so the size-ascending traversal incidentally surfaces the catalog of viable wire shapes (hoist-at-top → hoist-outside-Promise → internal-helper #10433 → DI-executor #10441 → two-site hoisted-check) without any explicit variant-coverage planning. Anti-pattern: picking the highest-LOC or highest-risk file first forces the most complex wire shape at campaign start and buries the simple shapes. Spawn-site count (N) as primary wire-shape predictor (#10445): LOC is the coarse first-pass sort; N is the actual shape selector — high-LOC files with N=1 take hoist-at-top regardless of LOC band (v872 311 LOC + v875 1440 LOC both N=1, both hoist-at-top). Router-with-conditional-bypass (#10447): when a router branches between gated and non-gated paths (e.g. installFromLocal vs installFromRemote), thread `ctx?` only into the gated branch; leave the bypass branch's signature unchanged — chokepoint scope is a property of the destination, not the router. Shared-helper hoist sub-variant catalog (#10448): five sub-variants from the v868-v882 12-chip campaign — hoist-at-top / two-site hoisted-check / class-instance two-site / internal-helper-method / module-internal-helper — plus a `module-singleton` carry-forward (1 instance v881, below promotion bar). Sub-variant selection is N-driven (cross-ref #10445); pick from the catalog rather than force-fitting.
codified_at_milestone: v1.49.784 (new domain — codified from v1.49.782 LoaderContext + v1.49.783 normalizer fix lesson cluster); v1.49.802 (extended with #10426 second-instance cross-class registry extraction from T1.1 arc); v1.49.847 (extended with #10440 production-caller path-narrowing from v845 + v846 two-instance evidence); v1.49.868 (extended with #10444 size-ascending chip-pick reveals wire-shape diversity from v858-v862 Process cluster + v863-v867 Egress cluster 10-chip two-cluster evidence); v1.49.883 (extended with #10445 spawn-site count as primary wire-shape predictor from v872 + v875 two-instance evidence + #10447 router-with-conditional-bypass from v864 + v880 two-instance evidence + #10448 shared-helper hoist sub-variant catalog with Track 5 wire-shape table from v868-v882 12-chip cluster)

trigger: When a handoff lists a fix as 'escalated' or 'load-bearing'; when a test in `tests/integration/` has been red across 2+ consecutive ships; when operator memory accumulates workarounds for a known broken tool
summary: Close escalated wedges within 1-2 milestones; do not defer beyond that. The cost of deferral is silent test drift + workaround documentation accumulation, not the eventual fix. Forward-cadence engine-state work can wait one milestone; an open red test in `tests/integration/` cannot. The pre-tag-gate's silent acceptance of a known-failing test is the alarm.
codified_at_milestone: v1.49.784 (new domain — codified from v1.49.783 STATE.md normalizer fix)

trigger: Authoring or extending a CLI tool in `tools/` that scans the codebase, emits metrics, or produces a comparable report; designing its test harness; piping its output to another command
summary: Test harnesses use spawnSync (not execSync) so stderr survives exit 0; cross-module analyzers scan multiple importer roots (not just src/); metric-emitting tools commit a baseline file so future runs diff via git; CLIs flush stdout before process.exit() to avoid 64KB pipe-buffer truncation; diff-emitting observability tools document the warm-up period (first run has no prior baseline); version-stamped baseline writers refuse to overwrite existing files with different content unless --force (v794 closure of v791 trip). #10421 field-validated at v789 by adoption-baseline diff emitting exactly the predicted line. Static-analysis tool parsers must handle common code-shape variants OR fail loudly (#10450): two real-world bugs in the same tool — v867 regex terminator inside comments (false negative) + v885 alias-stripping in named-import extractor (false positive). Sibling of #10427 failure-mode contracts: tools surfacing silent failures must not themselves fail silently. Mitigating discipline: sanity-fixtures exercise aliased imports / brackets-in-comments / namespace imports / dynamic imports / re-exports; inline assertions compare structural-view count against ground-truth count.
codified_at_milestone: v1.49.790 (new domain — codified from v1.49.785-789 adoption-telemetry build chain lesson cluster); v1.49.794 (extended with #10424 refuse-to-overwrite guard); v1.49.886 (extended with #10450 tool-parser-must-fail-loudly from v867+v885 two-instance evidence)

trigger: Authoring a per-module shelfware verdict (WIRED / RETIRED / ALLOWLISTED) for a module flagged by the adoption-scan as test-only; selecting the wire site that satisfies a WIRED verdict
summary: Observability surface (scanner + dashboard + allowlist) and decision surface (per-module verdicts) MUST live in separate files and evolve independently. Default to the lightest wire that flips the adoption-scan classification — a CLI subcommand usually suffices and preserves HARD-preservation invariants — defer richer in-loop wires to follow-on ships only if the verdict explicitly requires production use.
codified_at_milestone: v1.49.790 (new domain — codified from v1.49.789 first shelfware verdict lesson cluster)

trigger: Designing or extending the bounded-learning calibration loop in `src/bounded-learning/`; choosing the statistical primitive for an anytime-valid threshold-update decision; adding a new calibratable threshold class
summary: Two-sided likelihood-ratio e-processes on bounded binary observations at |x|=1 are insensitive to unanimous direction (cosh(λ)·exp(−λ²/2) ≤ 1 for all λ ≥ 0); use two one-sided e-processes at Bonferroni α/2 instead. Math-check-before-test-fixture convention: compute E_n/E_{n-1} under unanimous evidence by hand before writing the trip-point assertion — if the ratio is ≤ 1 the construction is unfit. Per-class observation sources are necessary surface; the lightest-wire discipline does NOT apply to necessary surface (cross-ref Architecture-retrofit patterns #10426). Calibrate-axis read-side wire recipe (#10451): 7-step pattern applied at v837 (predictive) + v884 (observation-retention) + v888 (token_budget.max_percent) — new events module mirror, dispatcher branch + wired-flag flip, public-API exports with alias if collision, CLI manual recorder dispatch + recorder function, read-side tests (~13), dispatcher tests with wired-flag flip + round-trip, CLI --summary count bump. STABLE at v888 (3 instances). Polarity convention matches threshold semantics (raise reduces fire-frequency → +1 favors lower); inverted for thresholds where raise INCREASES fire-frequency (predictive). Substrate-wrapper pattern (#10452): the substrate write-side ship follows a thin-function shape (`runX` function + minimal config interface + three-flag options bag + fire-and-forget auto-emit per #10437). Two sub-variants — default-fixed (v891 retention: substrate returns work-count, polarity is operator-intent, default kind is conservative bias) and outcome-driven (v893 ceiling-check: substrate IS a comparison, polarity falls out of the result). Discriminator: does the substrate's natural output determine polarity? Sibling of #10451 — #10451 is the read-side procedure, #10452 is the write-side procedure.
codified_at_milestone: v1.49.802 (new domain — codified from v1.49.795 T1.1 ship 1 e-process design lesson); v1.49.886 (extended with #10451 read-side wire recipe from v837+v884 two-instance evidence); v1.49.895 (extended with #10452 substrate-wrapper write-side pattern from v891+v893 two-instance evidence + #10451 STABLE promotion at v888 3rd instance)

trigger: Designing a new surface (function, module, CLI flag, skill prompt section, hook) where silent-vs-loud failure asymmetry is in play; adding a `try { ... } catch { /* ignore */ }` block; reviewing a long-running primitive's transient-error handling; adding a subscriber-gated observability hook on a context/options type
summary: Forensic, dashboard, and observability surfaces fail silently; load-bearing surfaces fail loudly. Test: does the user's next decision depend on this surface's output? If yes → load-bearing → propagate. If no → accessory → swallow at surface boundary. Contract documentation convention: explicit `// best-effort silent` or `// fail loudly` marker in docstring paired with at least one test assertion that exercises the failure path; surfaces without the paired test are stochastic. Subscriber-gated observability-only context-hook pattern (#10437): substrate-consumer hooks recur in a 5-element shape (optional `?` field + subscriber gate + fire-and-forget Promise + .catch(() => {}) swallow + two-argument derived-data signature). PAIR co-location (when multiple hooks live on the same surface) shares one try/catch to prevent per-hook drift. Re-throw ProcessContextDenied from CLI swallow-catch (#10442): CLI surfaces that absorb ALL errors into structured JSON output must re-throw `ProcessContextDenied` as the first line of the catch block — security denials are load-bearing per #10427 and must propagate even when operational errors are absorbed for UX. Multi-catch helper (#10446): when a chokepoint campaign chips many call sites across both ProcessContext AND EgressContext (~30 instances by v882), the per-catch `if (err instanceof XContextDenied) throw err;` boilerplate consolidates into one helper — `rethrowIfDenied(err)` (inline form for catch blocks with bespoke reporting) or `callOrRethrowDenial(fn)` (higher-order wrapper for catch blocks that collapse to `undefined`). One helper, one source of truth for which denials propagate; future class additions (LoaderContextDenied) update one place. Fire-and-forget test-side wait via `setTimeout(50ms)` (#10454): when asserting on a fire-and-forget Promise's side effects, the test-side wait MUST use a real timeout (50ms is enough for typical mkdir + appendFile on real disk), NOT `setImmediate(resolve)` or `process.nextTick` — those advance only the event loop, while OS-level fs operations require wall-clock time. v891 + v893 + v894 three-instance evidence. Limitation: the `.catch(() => {})` swallow is correct-by-construction; tests can't reliably trigger the failure path without OS-level setup, so the protection is structural rather than test-verified.
codified_at_milestone: v1.49.802 (new domain — codified from v1.49.799-801 three-instance audit-log + watch-loop + /sc:status lesson cluster); v1.49.840 (extended with #10437 subscriber-gated context-hook pattern from v810+v826 onPredictions + v830+v832 fallbackProvider four-instance evidence set + PAIR co-location refinement); v1.49.847 (extended with #10442 re-throw ProcessContextDenied from CLI swallow-catch from v820 + v842 two-instance evidence); v1.49.883 (extended with #10446 multi-catch helper from ~30-instance evidence across v868-v882 Track 4+5 chokepoint clusters); v1.49.895 (extended with #10454 fire-and-forget test-side wait pattern from v891+v893+v894 three-instance evidence)

trigger: Planning a counter-cadence cleanup-mission; deciding which operational debt to spend a non-degree-advancing ship on; auditing whether the project's four operational axes (codify / consume / calibrate / verify) are in balance
summary: Four operational axes — codify (~7-10 ships), consume (≤6 ships from substrate to first non-test caller), calibrate (~10 ships per wired threshold tick), verify (~10 ships per substrate-with-callers-but-no-integration-test; #10438 promoted at v847 from v829+v832 evidence). Cadence-overdue check is prose-only and informational: each axis names its overdue trigger; the operator picks; the next counter-cadence's scope discussion NAMES the proposed axis even if a different axis wins on operator priority. CLI manual + substrate auto-emit duality (#10439): a calibratable threshold's loop is structurally incomplete until BOTH write callers ship — a CLI manual recorder (operator-attributed events with explicit polarity) AND a substrate auto-recorder (traffic-attributed events with a default polarity that mirrors the CLI default). Plan THREE ships per threshold: observation-source registration → CLI manual recorder → substrate auto-recorder. Substrate→calibration end-to-end integration test pattern (#10453): the verify-axis closing-move test shape — temp dir setup, substrate write, fire-and-forget wait via setTimeout(50ms) per #10454, calibration-loop read, polarity assertion (single + multi-event with net-sum check), missing-file tolerance, malformed-line tolerance. v856 predictive low-confidence + v894 observation-retention two-instance evidence. Mirror v856 or v894 as the skeleton when filing the integration-test ship; verify-axis budget extends 10 ships from substrate ship version.
codified_at_milestone: v1.49.805 (new domain — codified from S3 lever in 2026-05-26 core-functions audit retrospective); v1.49.844 (verify-axis added as canonical-doc home for v829+v832 evidence base); v1.49.847 (numbered-lesson promotions: #10438 verify axis + #10439 CLI manual + substrate auto-emit duality from v803 + v845/v846 evidence); v1.49.895 (extended with #10453 substrate→calibration end-to-end integration test pattern from v856+v894 two-instance evidence)

trigger: Authoring docs that surface opt-in flags to operators; reviewing whether a new substrate ship needs a public-surface entry; deciding what a new operator's second-30-minutes path should look like
summary: Per-substrate four-field shape: what it unlocks / what it costs / opt-in mechanic / when to defer. Public surface (this doc) is the WHY-truth source; `docs/MODULE-DEFAULTS.md` is the flag-truth source. Add new entries below their axis grouping (observability / learning / execution / safety) in ship order. Retire entries only on codebase removal, not on default-flip (note inline). Cross-link to the canonical flag-truth doc; do NOT duplicate flag tables.
codified_at_milestone: v1.49.805 (new domain — codified from S4 lever in 2026-05-26 core-functions audit retrospective)

trigger: Adding a new src/ caller that reads from disk, issues a network request, or spawns a child process; reviewing whether a new module needs to thread an existing `ctx?` parameter; migrating a KNOWN_UNWIRED entry from one of the audit-test allowlists
summary: Three sibling chokepoints — LoaderContext (filesystem, v782), EgressContext (network egress, v806), ProcessContext (child-process spawn, v806) — share a common shape (optional `ctx?` param, allow-list pattern, audit sink, `*ContextDenied` error) but specialize their targets. Each chokepoint's audit-test (`src/security/*-context-audit.test.ts`) is the runtime enforcement: greps src/ for the surface's signature, accepts either `ensure*Allowed(` calls or `Role: NOT a <surface> caller` docstring exemption, grandfathered existing call sites tracked in KNOWN_UNWIRED. Security denials are load-bearing per #10427 — hoist `ensure*Allowed(` OUTSIDE try/catch blocks that swallow errors. Internal-helper pattern (#10433): when a file already wraps the side-effecting op in an internal helper, thread `ctx?` through the helper for 1 LOC × N callsites; files without a helper take N LOC × N callsites. DI-executor + tokenized-argv wire shape (#10441): when a module exposes a factory with an optional injected executor for testability, the default executor closes over `ctx?`, tokenizes the cmd string to extract executable + argv, and calls `ensureProcessAllowed` before delegating — a sub-class of #10433 where the default executor IS the internal helper. execFile vs shell-exec audit target accuracy (#10449): direct-exec (`execFile`) records the actual binary in the audit log; shell-exec (`exec`) records `sh`. Prefer direct-exec for new ProcessContext wires when no shell features (pipes, globs, redirection, variable expansion) are required — tighter allow-list (admits only the named binary class), greppable audit records, narrower blast radius from over-grants. Tokenize cmd strings with `trim().split(/\s+/)` per #10441's tokenize step.
codified_at_milestone: v1.49.806 (new domain — codified from S6 lever in 2026-05-26 core-functions audit retrospective; extends #10414 LoaderContext pattern to network egress + child-process spawn surfaces); v1.49.824 (extended with #10433 internal-helper pattern for ctx? threading from v809 + v820 case studies); v1.49.847 (extended with #10441 DI-executor + tokenized-argv wire shape from v825 + v843 three-instance evidence); v1.49.883 (extended with #10449 execFile vs shell-exec audit target accuracy from v853 + v874 two-instance evidence)

trigger: Designing a closure for a drift class that originates from a multi-step operator procedure OR from a tool blindly overwriting destination files that may contain hand-authored content; reviewing whether a one-layer fix (detector OR source-eliminator alone) is structurally complete; planning a counter-cadence ship targeting an operational-debt class
summary: Procedure-rooted drift classes (where an operator-discretion step is forgettable/skippable) require BOTH a source eliminator (deterministic tool replacing the discretion step + post-condition check) AND a detector gate (structural check catching bypass). Either layer alone is incomplete: source-without-detector lets future operators work around the tool; detector-without-source leaves the human-error window open. Case study: STATE.md v805→v806 drift closed in v807 (detector: pre-tag-gate step 0.5) + v813 (source: atomic-writer `tools/state-md-set-shipped.mjs`). Generalizes to file-overwrite drift (#10436): v836 publish.mjs `shouldPublishToDestination` is the destination-side preservation layer paired with chapter.mjs's source-side preservation (from v585 C04). The manual recovery procedure operators worked around for 251 ships (`git checkout HEAD -- <chapters>`) was the alarm bell for a missing destination-side detector. Apply at every counter-cadence ship targeting a known procedure-rooted or file-overwrite drift class.
codified_at_milestone: v1.49.814 (new domain — codified from v807+v813 case study; extends #10414 gate-not-vigilance to procedure-rooted drift specifically); v1.49.840 (extended with #10436 file-overwrite drift sub-class from v813 STATE.md + v836 publish.mjs two-instance evidence)

trigger: Introducing a new audit-test that enforces a cross-cutting invariant on src/ files with grandfathered call sites; reviewing how to grandfather existing call sites without permanently exempting them; planning chip-down ships against an existing allowlist; consolidating allowlist entries when N siblings are all wired; adding inverse-audit coverage for a newly-surfaced stale-entry shape
summary: When a chokepoint or cross-cutting audit-test is introduced into a mature codebase, it MUST grandfather existing call sites via a named allowlist in the audit-test itself. The allowlist is debt, not exemption — should asymptote toward zero as chip ships consume it. Per-ship release notes record `<surface> KNOWN_UNWIRED N → N-K`. Established pattern at v806 (initial 16 egress + 38 process allowlist entries) + v809+v811+v812 (3 chip ships demonstrating the migration cadence). Generalization beyond chokepoints (#10434): the same ratchet-ledger shape works for any cross-cutting observability+enforcement surface where the current state has N enumerated out-of-conformance entries and each is independently chip-able — v821 discipline-coverage ceiling generalizes the chokepoint KNOWN_UNWIRED pattern to per-discipline UNCODIFIED counts. Inverse-audit stale-entry detection (#10443): the v806 audit-tests are unidirectional — they enforce 'files NOT in KNOWN_UNWIRED but containing the signature MUST call ensure*Allowed' but do NOT enforce the inverse. Two distinct stale-shapes have accumulated under that asymmetry — Shape A (wired-but-still-in-allowlist, surfaced v834) closed by v838 inline inverse-checks; Shape B (import-without-use, surfaced v852) closed by v857 cross-audit tool `tools/security/check-stale-known-unwired.mjs`. The discipline scales by adding one inverse-check per stale-shape; a third shape extends the catalog rather than creating a new discipline. Continuous-verification mode (refinement of #10443 operationalized v1.49.858-867): invoke the cross-audit tool after each chip ship as the third step in build → verify → ship — 10 consecutive applications across Process+Egress clusters surfaced the tool's first real-world parser bug at v867 (regex hardened with line-anchor + multi-line flag). Sibling discipline: tools designed to surface silent-vs-loud asymmetry must themselves fail loudly — a vacuous-true tool is worse than no tool. Add sanity-check assertions comparing tool's structural-view count against the actual allowlist file's enumerated entries.
codified_at_milestone: v1.49.814 (new domain — codified from v806 introduction + v809+v811+v812 chip-ship demonstration; companion to Security chokepoints discipline); v1.49.824 (extended with #10434 ratchet-ledger generalization from v821 discipline-coverage ceiling case study); v1.49.857 (extended with #10443 inverse-audit stale-entry-detection from v834 Shape A + v852 Shape B two-instance evidence; cross-audit tool tools/security/check-stale-known-unwired.mjs); v1.49.868 (extended with #10443 continuous-verification mode operational discipline from v858-v867 10-consecutive-application evidence + v867 first-real-world-bug surfaced/fixed in-flight)

trigger: Authoring a wire between src/ and .college/ (or any future rootdir pair) where the two rootdirs cannot import each other directly; declaring duck-typed interfaces in one rootdir + satisfying structurally in the other; verifying the wire works end-to-end at the application boundary
summary: Cross-rootdir wires follow a 4-step pattern: (1) declare the interface in the CONSUMER rootdir with minimal primitive field types; (2) implement the satisfier in the PROVIDER rootdir via duck typing (no `implements` keyword; no cross-import); (3) bridge via an optional context field that's no-op when unset; (4) verify with a `tests/integration/*.integration.test.ts` test that has visibility into BOTH rootdirs. Two subordinate patterns: local-interface redeclaration discipline (when the satisfier RETURNS a typed value, the provider rootdir must locally redeclare the result type byte-equivalently — drift between the two declarations is silent), and `Pick<T, K>` structural-handle narrowing (when constructor opts accept concrete classes, type them as `Pick<>` so tests can pass thin mocks without a cast while real instances still flow through subtyping).
codified_at_milestone: v1.49.833 (new domain — codified from 5-instance evidence set v823 SkillActivationObserver declaration + v829 application-boundary verification + v830 ConceptFallbackProvider framework + v831 RosettaConceptFallback implementation + v832 selector 2nd-caller wire + tests/integration/ verification)
